title: Enabling superior impact toughness of low-density δ-ferrite steel by dispersing ultra-fine spheroidized carbides in the ferrite/carbide composite lamellae
abstract_summary: This study systematically investigates the effect of rolling temperature on microstructure evolution and impact toughness of low-density δ-ferrite steel. High-temperature rolling (850–950°C) produces a lamellar microstructure with honeycomb martensite and DIFT grains, resulting in low impact energy (<7 J). Low-temperature rolling (650–750°C) creates an alternating δ-ferrite/carbide composite lamellae with ultra-fine spherical carbides, achieving high impact energy (207.5 J) through Orowan strengthening, crack blunting, crack deflection, and microvoid-induced crack propagation. This work provides a new strategy for strength-toughness regulation in lightweight steels.
research_objective: To elucidate the synergistic effect of rolling temperature on microstructure and fracture toughness in low-density δ-ferrite steel and to reveal the mechanisms by which ultra-fine spherical carbides in lamellar structures enhance impact resistance.
material_systems: Phases: δ-ferrite, martensite, ultra-fine spherical carbides (cementite), deformation-induced ferrite (DIF)
expected_information_axis: microstructure (lamellar morphology, carbide size distribution, phase fractions, grain structures)
property (impact toughness, tensile strength, elongation, hardness)
mechanism (Orowan strengthening, crack tip blunting, crack deflection, microvoid nucleation and horizontal crack propagation)
figure_evidence (TEM, HRTEM, EBSD, SEM fractography, schematics)
key_sections: 1. Introduction: Motivates toughness challenge in low-density δ-ferrite steel and introduces the concept of combining lamellar structure with carbide reinforcement; states research gap and objectives.
2. Experimental materials and methods: Describes material preparation, rolling processes (temperature conditions R650-R950), mechanical tests and microstructure characterization techniques.
3. Results: Microstructural observation (3.1) and mechanical properties (3.2) across different rolling temperatures.

4. Discussion: Synergistic effect of rolling temperature on microstructure (4.1) and influence of microstructure on impact and tensile behaviors (4.2); details Orowan mechanism, fracture modes, and crack propagation.
5. Conclusions: Summarizes the toughening mechanisms and the path for strength-toughness regulation.
